findings: Independent verification confirms Red-tailed Hawk (Buteo jamaicensis), family Accipitridae. The measurements shown are valid combined sex ranges: length 43–66 cm (17–26 in), wingspan 114–133 cm (45–52 in), and weight 690–1,470 g (24–52 oz).

The plate accurately depicts a light/adult-type Red-tailed Hawk: one head, one strongly hooked bill, two folded wings, two yellow-legged/footed legs, and one cinnamon-red tail. It has broad buteo proportions, brown upperparts, pale streaked underparts, and a darker belly band consistent with the references and verified field marks.

Visible text is consistent with the verified species’ behavior, open-country/edge habitat, adult versus juvenile tail distinction, variable morphs, and underwing pattern. No material species or text errors found.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Red-tailed Hawk Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
